channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Miséria eleitoral
item.description: The PSD has decided that Ronaldo Caiado will be its presidential candidate in 2026, but his nomination hinges on a rapid rise in polling numbers to remain viable. Without such growth, the decision may be reversed due to concerns over electoral competitiveness.

item.title: Eleição será polarizada e Caiado deve ficar à margem na disputa pela Presidência, diz Gleisi
item.description: Minister Gleisi Hoffmann stated that the 2026 presidential elections will be polarized and that Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) should remain on the periphery of the main contest. She suggested Caiado will not play a central role in the race for the Presidency.

item.title: Presidente do PT de SP defende Kassab na vice de Lula e Alckmin para Senado
item.description: The president of the Workers' Party in São Paulo, federal deputy Kiko Celeguim, advocates for Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD and former mayor, to replace Geraldo Alckmin as Lula's running mate in the 2026 presidential ticket. Alckmin would then run for the Senate. The proposal signals a potential political realignment among the PT, PSD, and PSB to strengthen the governing coalition.

item.title: Eduardo Leite reage ao anúncio da pré-candidatura de Ronaldo Caiado
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul, indirectly criticized the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its 2026 presidential pre-candidate, stating the decision deepens radicalized political polarization in Brazil, though he did not announce leaving the party. He emphasized that the pursuit of balance and common sense in politics will continue in society and reaffirmed his commitment to public service.

item.title: Quaest: Flávio Bolsonaro vence Lula entre eleitores do Espírito Santo
item.description: A Quaest poll shows Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) leading Lula (PT) in the presidential race in Espírito Santo, with 37% to 26%. Other candidates remain below 4%, while 16% are undecided and 14% would vote blank/null or not vote. The survey also found that 49% of Espírito Santo residents view the federal government negatively.

item.title: STF define semana que vem se eleição no Rio será direta ou indireta; entenda como fica o governo
item.description: Minister Edson Fachin has scheduled an in-person STF hearing for April 8 to decide whether Rio's interim gubernatorial election will be indirect (via the state legislature) or direct (by popular vote). The ruling aims to uphold constitutional legality, legal security, and institutional stability, with the winner serving until January 1, 2027. The PSD, led by Eduardo Paes, filed the action to prevent Douglas Ruas from being elected indirectly by the ALERJ, which would benefit his PL rival.

item.title: 'Tende a manter polarização', diz Eduardo Leite sobre escolha de Caiado como nome ao Planalto
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite criticized the PSD's decision to select Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, stating the choice "disenchants". Leite, also a contender for the party's nomination, argued the decision maintains political polarization.

item.title: Kassab sobre candidatos do PSD nos Estados: vai ter quem apoie Lula, vai ter quem apoie Caiado
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab said he expects party figures to support President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) in the 2026 elections, rather than backing the party's own presidential candidate, Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado. The statement suggests a potential internal split within the PSD over presidential support in the upcoming elections.

item.title: Kassab: equilíbrio fiscal foi comprometido com Lula; Bolsonaro teve ações lamentáveis na saúde
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, criticized Lula's government for compromising fiscal balance and Bolsonaro's administration for lamentable actions in health, while commenting on Ronaldo Caiado's presidential candidacy for the PSD in 2026.

item.title: Ratinho Jr. celebra escolha de Caiado como candidato à Presidência pelo PSD
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior celebrated on social media the selection of Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as the PSD's presidential candidate, after withdrawing from the race to remain in office. He praised Caiado's nationally recognized work in education and security, and Eduardo Leite's fiscal recovery of Rio Grande do Sul, framing Caiado as a right-wing alternative to oppose Lula's government.

item.title: A resposta do governador do Maranhão ao seu pedido de afastamento no STF
item.description: Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão responded to a PCdoB petition before the STF seeking his removal over alleged nepotism and non-compliance with rulings by Minister Alexandre de Moraes. Brandão argued the move is politically motivated, stemming from the party’s break with his administration, and criticized the judicialization of partisan disputes. The background includes his rift with former governor and now STF Justice Flávio Dino. The PGR will weigh in before the relator’s decision.

item.title: Brasil vai ser melhor se Caiado for presidente, afirma Kassab
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, national president of the PSD, announced Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) as the party's presidential candidate for 2026. He stated that Caiado has the courage, energy, and experience to take on commitments, adding that 'Brazil will be better if Caiado is president'.

item.title: Ratinho Jr. elogia escolha de Caiado para disputar Presidência pelo PSD: ‘Fonte de inspiração’
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior praised the PSD's decision to nominate Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, citing Caiado's national reputation in education and security as an inspiration. He also acknowledged Eduardo Leite’s public service, though Leite later expressed disappointment with the party’s decision and criticized traditional Brazilian politics.

item.title: Bolsonaro tem mais restrições que Collor em prisão domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes granted humanitarian house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro with stricter measures than those applied to Fernando Collor in 2025, including bans on cell phones, social media, and direct or indirect communication, plus limits on visits and gatherings. Bolsonaro's measure has an initial 90-day term with review, while Collor's is unlimited and based solely on age and health without communication restrictions.

item.title: Tarcísio venceria aliados de Lula em SP, diz AtlasIntel
item.description: An AtlasIntel/Estado de S.Paulo poll shows that Governor Tarcísio de Freitas would win all four second-round scenarios tested against Lula-allied candidates, with leads ranging from 8.3 to 18.3 percentage points. His largest advantage is over Fernando Haddad (PT), at 53.5% to 43.2%. The survey interviewed 2,200 people in São Paulo from March 24–29, 2026, with a 2-point margin of error.

item.title: BTG/Nexus: Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro empatam em 1º e 2º turnos
item.description: Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) are tied in both first and second round scenarios for the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, according to the first wave of the BTG/Nexus poll. The technical tie reflects enduring polarization in the Brazilian electorate, with no clear advantage for either leading candidate tested. The poll was released on March 30, 2026, at 12:12 PM.

item.title: Kassab diz que PSD escolheu Caiado por ter mais chances de ir ao 2º turno
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, stated that the party chose Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential candidate due to his higher likelihood of reaching the runoff compared to Eduardo Leite. Nevertheless, both candidates trail behind Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in voter intention polls. Leite publicly opposed the decision, arguing it reinforces a radically polarized political environment.

item.title: ACM Neto fecha chapa com PL e deve apoiar Flávio Bolsonaro na Bahia
item.description: ACM Neto has closed a ticket with the PL to run for governor of Bahia, featuring João Roma and Ângelo Coronel as Senate candidates and Zé Cocá as vice-governor. The ticket will openly support Flávio Bolsonaro for president, mirroring national polarization between PT and bolsonarism in the state. Coronel’s departure from the PSD after backing Lula weakens Ronaldo Caiado’s presidential prospects in Bahia.

item.title: Palanques estaduais expõem forças e limites de Lula e Flávio na disputa de 2026
item.description: Lula maintains consolidated support from Northeastern governors but faces declining popularity in the region, while Flávio Bolsonaro intensifies his pre-campaign in the Northeast and strengthens alliances in the South and Center-West to expand his state-level base. The battle for governors' endorsements is becoming decisive in a still fragmented landscape, seven months ahead of the 2026 presidential election.

item.title: Kassab aposta em Caiado por perfil mais combativo e governo já prepara estratégia contra candidatura
item.description: Gilberto Kassab, president of the PSD, is backing Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás, for his more combative profile to capture votes from center and non-aligned right-wing voters currently supporting Flávio Bolsonaro. Caiado’s strategy will focus on three pillars: hardline public security, fiscally responsible liberal economics, and social policies strengthened by his medical background. The Lula government is preparing a response, maintaining its strategy against Bolsonaro but adjusting rhetoric to link Caiado to hardline bolsonarism and conservative allies, with discussions underway about creating a dedicated public security ministry.

item.title: Dívida continua “sustentável” em todos os cenários, diz Tesouro
item.description: National Treasury Secretary Daniel Leal stated that Brazil’s public debt remains sustainable across all projected scenarios, with stabilization expected around 84% of GDP in the coming years. Despite a primary deficit of R$30.1 billion in February and record interest payments of R$1.03 trillion over 12 months, the government emphasizes fiscal transparency and attributes rising debt service costs to the Central Bank’s contractionary monetary policy, while affirming debt sustainability is not compromised.

item.title: Preterido no PSD, Leite perde disputa interna por candidatura pela 2ª vez
item.description: Eduardo Leite suffered his second consecutive defeat in the PSD's internal presidential candidacy dispute, following Ronaldo Caiado's selection as the party's representative. Leite criticized the decision for perpetuating radical polarization and reaffirmed his commitment to a genuine liberal democratic center, without clarifying whether he will remain in office or run for another position in 2026. In 2022, he had similarly sought to launch a presidential bid via the PSDB but lost the party's primaries to João Doria and Arthur Virgílio.

item.title: Diretor-geral da PF diz que corporação ‘não protege nem persegue’: ‘A gente trabalha com isenção’
item.description: Federal Police Director-General Andrei Rodrigues stated that the agency neither protects nor persecutes anyone and operates with complete impartiality, denying any political influence in its investigations. He highlighted progress in the Banco Master case and praised the work of Central Bank President Gabriel Galípolo and Coaf head Ricardo Saadi, despite recent criticism of the agency and restrictions imposed by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on the sharing of financial intelligence reports.

item.title: Crusoé: Decisão ‘tende a manter um ambiente de polarização radicalizada’, diz Leite
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite stated that the PSD's decision to nominate Ronaldo Caiado for president tends to maintain a radicalized polarization environment in the country. Leite, who was overlooked by the party, said he would not discuss the decision but reiterated his concern about the current political landscape. The remarks were made in a video on Monday, the 30th.

item.title: Meio-Dia em Brasília: Baixaria no fim da CPMI do INSS
item.description: The Meio-Dia em Brasília program discussed the emptied week in the Chamber of Deputies due to the end of the partisan window and the departure of Lula government ministers to run in the 2026 elections. It also covered the conclusion of the INSS CPMI, which investigated alleged irregularities in the institute, sparking debate over public management and potential collusion between political power and pension benefits.

item.title: Michelle Bolsonaro assiste posse de Celina Leão da primeira fileira
item.description: Former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro attended the inauguration of Celina Leão as governor of the Federal District from the front row of the Legislative Chamber's auditorium. During her speech, Leão praised Bolsonaro and former President Jair Bolsonaro, expressing hope for an end to Brazil's challenges. Michelle, a pre-candidate for Senate in the DF, has already declared her support for Leão in the gubernatorial race.

item.title: Eduardo Leite critica escolha de Caiado como candidato a presidente: ‘Tende a manter polarização’
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite criticized the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, saying the decision tends to maintain the country's radicalized polarization. Leite advocated for a true liberal democratic center based on dialogue and conciliation, emphasizing he does not rule out a future candidacy, as politics is dynamic.

item.title: ‘Não sei fazer PPT’: Paulo Vieira ironiza, em rede nacional, infográfico que associou Lula a Vorcaro
item.description: Comedian Paulo Vieira mocked GloboNews' error in displaying an infographic that falsely associated President Lula with Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, during the 'Melhores do Ano' program. He joked that he 'doesn't know how to make PPTs' but used the quip to criticize the unfounded link between Lula and Vorcaro, emphasizing the humorous tone and his affiliation with the network. Vieira also satirized the Rio de Janeiro government succession after Cláudio Castro's resignation, joking that the governorship is handed to anyone arriving at the airport.

item.title: Moraes cobra explicação sobre vídeo citado por Eduardo Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes ordered Bolsonaro's defense to provide clarifications within 24 hours regarding a possible violation of house arrest measures, following statements by Eduardo Bolsonaro about recording a video.

item.title: Caiado será outro Bolsonaro na sucessão presidencial de 2026
item.description: Gilberto Kassab suffered two setbacks in his preparation for the 2026 presidential race, undermining his centrist strategy and pushing him toward a more right-wing alignment. He abandoned Eduardo Leite in favor of Ronaldo Caiado as a Bolsonaro-aligned alternative. The text implies that conservative voters prefer candidates associated with Bolsonaro’s legacy, viewing Caiado as a successor rather than an original.

item.title: ‘Desencanto’: a crítica de Eduardo Leite após o PSD escolher Caiado na eleição
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite expressed disappointment with the PSD's decision to nominate Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as its 2026 presidential candidate, stating the choice risks perpetuating a radicalized polarization in Brazil. While he refrained from directly challenging the decision, Leite reiterated his commitment to a liberal center and argued that political progress continues through societal ideas and actions, expressing confidence that Brazil will eventually regain its path toward balance.

item.title: Moraes dá 24h para Bolsonaro explicar vídeo citado por Eduardo
item.description: Minister Alexandre de Moraes gave Jair Bolsonaro 24 hours to explain whether he watched a video recorded by his son Eduardo Bolsonaro at CPAC, in which Eduardo said he was showing the footage to his father to prove he cannot be silenced. Moraes reiterated the ban on Bolsonaro using social media directly or through third parties, and on communicating with the exterior, including via relatives. The ruling comes as Bolsonaro serves a 90-day temporary house arrest due to severe bilateral bacterial pneumonia, following 14 days of hospitalization.

item.title: Leite revela ‘desencanto’ e critica PSD por manter radicalização política ao escolher Caiado; veja
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite expressed disappointment with the PSD's decision to formally confirm Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, criticizing the party for perpetuating extreme political polarization in Brazil. Although institutionally legitimate, Leite argues the choice reinforces a divisive political climate that hinders national development and reveals his frustration after hoping for a non-polarized alternative. He confirmed he will complete his gubernatorial term until December 2026 and will not run for another office this year, despite strong polling for a Senate run.

item.title: Leite diz que PSD mantém “polarização radicalizada” ao escolher Caiado
item.description: Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD-RS) criticized the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, arguing it perpetuates a 'radicalized polarization' that stifles national debate. While not contesting the party's decision, Leite advocated for a centrist, liberal democratic alternative, citing broad support from political leaders, economists, and civil society for a more balanced political project.

item.title: Eduardo Leite cita desencanto em crítica ao PSD por lançar Caiado ao Planalto
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite criticized the PSD's decision to nominate Ronaldo Caiado as a presidential candidate, stating the choice fuels radicalized polarization in Brazil. While expressing disappointment with the party's move, Leite clarified it does not reflect a lack of conviction, thanked supporters, and affirmed he will remain in office until the end of his term.

item.title: Lula tem 45% de aprovação e 51% de desaprovação, diz BTG/Nexus
item.description: Lula has 45% approval and 51% disapproval in the BTG/Nexus poll, yielding a net negative six-point balance. Despite majority disapproval, his 35% positive government rating sits at the historical floor for reeleeligibility, preserving his competitiveness in a polarized context.

item.title: Fachin aposta em estratégia de caso dos penduricalhos para resolver eleição no RJ
item.description: STF President Edson Fachin is seeking consensus among ministers to determine the election method for Rio de Janeiro's governor after Claudio Castro's resignation, aiming to prevent the continuation of an indirect election scheme via Alerj. The talks include TSE President Cármen Lúcia and other justices, following the successful strategy used in the 'penduricalhos' case. The PSD's injunctions are set for judgment on April 8, focusing on whether the election should be direct or indirect.

item.title: A fala de Eduardo Bolsonaro nos EUA que complica o pai, em prisão domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes ordered the defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro to clarify, within 24 hours, a statement made by Eduardo Bolsonaro in the U.S., in which he said he was recording a video to show his father, who is under house arrest. The remark was interpreted as a potential attempt to circumvent the ban on external communication imposed on the former president, as sending messages through third parties is also prohibited under the precautionary measures. Moraes indicated the episode may constitute a violation of the conditions imposed on Bolsonaro.

item.title: Quem é Ronaldo Caiado, governador de Goiás e pré-candidato à Presidência pelo PSD
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado, has been chosen by the PSD as the party's pre-candidate for the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, following the withdrawal of Ratinho Júnior. The decision ends an internal contest with Eduardo Leite and comes after a long political career that includes federal deputy, senator, and governor roles. Caiado previously ran for president in 1989 under the PSD, receiving less than 1% of the vote.

item.title: PSD dá exemplo de democracia ao escolher Caiado, diz Ratinho Junior
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior (PSD) praised the selection of Ronaldo Caiado as the party's presidential pre-candidate, emphasizing the democratic process and Caiado's administrative experience in education and security. The decision followed Ratinho Junior's withdrawal from the internal race, boosting Caiado's standing, while Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite, also a contender, was sidelined and criticized the choice for deepening political polarization, though he said he would not challenge the party's decision.

item.title: Leite não cita Caiado, diz estar desencantado e que a escolha do PSD mantém a radicalização polarizada no Brasil
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite expressed disappointment with the PSD's decision to nominate Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential candidate, stating the choice perpetuates Brazil's polarized radicalization. He said he would not debate the decision but reaffirmed his commitment to democracy, balance, and reason in politics. Leite had previously shown enthusiasm for Caiado's potential candidacy but adopted a tone of disillusionment after the party's formal announcement.

item.title: Lula e Flávio empatam no 1º e 2º turno, diz Nexus
item.description: The Nexus poll released on March 30, 2026, shows a technical tie between Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) in both the first and second rounds of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election, with Lula ranging from 39% to 42% and Flávio from 38% to 39% in the first round, and both at 46% in a hypothetical runoff. The 2-point margin of error indicates no statistically significant lead. Lula faces 51% disapproval of his government and 49% rejection, while Flávio has 48% rejection.

item.title: ‘PSD escolheu homem aprovado como gestor’, diz Ratinho Jr sobre Caiado candidato
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. praised the selection of Ronaldo Caiado as the PSD's presidential candidate for 2026, highlighting Caiado's strong approval ratings in Goiás, particularly in education and security, and his vision as a third way in the polarized race between Lula and Bolsonaro. He also acknowledged Eduardo Leite of Rio Grande do Sul for his fiscal management and national repositioning of the state.

item.title: A reação de Ratinho Junior à escolha do PSD de lançar Caiado para presidente
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior publicly reacted to the PSD's decision to nominate Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential candidate, praising the move as an example of democratic commitment and highlighting Caiado's nationally recognized management in education and security. He also congratulated fellow PSD contender Eduardo Leite, acknowledging his stature and public spirit. Ratinho Junior previously announced he would not run for office in 2026, concluding his term in Paraná.

item.title: STF julgará eleição indireta ao governo do Rio em 8 de abril
item.description: The STF has scheduled April 8 for a definitive ruling on whether the vacancy left by Cláudio Castro's resignation will be filled by direct or indirect election in Rio de Janeiro. Until then, TJ-RJ President Ricardo Couto de Castro will remain in office on an interim basis. Minister Cristiano Zanin requested a review to unify the Court's understanding, citing a divergence with a TSE precedent from Tocantins in 2018, which opted for direct election in a similar scenario.

item.title: 'Exemplo de compromisso com a democracia', diz Ratinho Junior sobre escolha de Caiado como presidenciável pelo PSD
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior announced his support for Ronaldo Caiado as the PSD’s presidential candidate in 2026, after withdrawing his own bid. He praised Caiado as a nationally recognized manager, particularly in education and security, and highlighted the party’s democratic commitment through an internal balanced debate. He also acknowledged Eduardo Leite’s fiscal leadership and national impact, praising his role in repositioning Rio Grande do Sul.

item.title: Moraes manda Bolsonaro explicar publicação de Eduardo para avaliar violação em domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has requested that Jair Bolsonaro's defense explain a social media post made by Eduardo Bolsonaro to assess whether it violates the ban on social media use imposed as part of the former president's house arrest. The ruling aims to determine if the content shared by Bolsonaro's son constitutes a breach of the precautionary measure.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Articulações eleitorais têm semana decisiva com saídas de cargos e fim da janela partidária
item.description: This week is decisive for electoral articulations in Brazil, with at least 18 Lula government ministers expected to step down by Saturday to run in the October elections, particularly focusing on Rio de Janeiro, where a June gubernatorial race is gaining traction. The CPMI on INSS concluded, provoking reactions in the STF, while Alexandre de Moraes issued a decision limiting the use of Coaf financial reports. Ministerial replacements are still being defined, notably the uncertainty over Gleisi Hoffmann’s succession in the political coordination role.

item.title: Eduardo Leite avisa ao PSD que vai concluir mandato de governador após ser preterido por Caiado
item.description: Eduardo Leite announced he will remain as governor of Rio Grande do Sul until the end of his term in December 2026, after being passed over for the presidential nomination by the PSD, which selected Ronaldo Caiado as its candidate. Leite had switched from the PSDB to the PSD to pursue a candidacy outside the PT-Bolsonarista polarization, but failed to gain expected traction in polls. Although he performs well in Senate race polls, he chose not to run for another office in 2026, as agreed with the party.

item.title: Lula lidera no 1º turno e empata com Flávio Bolsonaro no 2º, mostra BTG/Nexus
item.description: The BTG/Nexus poll shows Lula (PT) leading in the first round with 41%, technically tied with Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) at 38%, while all other candidates remain below 5%. In the runoff, both are tied at 46% each, with 7% blank/null votes, reflecting nearly identical rejection rates (49% and 48%) and a highly competitive, open election likely to be decided by narrow margins and voter persuasion.
